title: platform done catalog INDEX row
purpose: D6.1 defines platform done catalog index row for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D6.1` only; siblings under `D6` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: Platform promotion is not done until a catalog entry exists and an INDEX row points to the artifact—playbook slug, script path, skill name, or pack fragment id discoverable from compose-first search.
After artifact creation, [D4.5](D4.5-platform-work-catalog-regeneration.md) regeneration often completes the promotion by updating INDEX and manifest catalog nodes.
Each row records maturity level, verify command reference, and optional promotion id trace so audit can reopen incomplete promotions.
Librarian `allowed_reads` and [B4.3](B4.3-compose-first-catalog-before-improvise.md) treat INDEX as authoritative—consumer goals must not hunt ad-hoc paths omitted from INDEX.

Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.

Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D6.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D6.1` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D6.1` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D6.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D6.1 defines platform done catalog index row for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system platform promotion is not done until a catalog entry exists and an index row points to the artifact—playbook slug, script path, skill name, or pack fragment id discoverable from compose-first search, then after artifact creation, [d4.5](d4.5-platform-work-catalog-regeneration.md) regeneration often completes the promotion by updating index and manifest catalog nodes.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

Platform promotion is not done until a catalog entry exists and INDEX row points to it—playbook slug, script path, skill name, or pack fragment id discoverable from compose-first search. INDEX regeneration ([D4.5](D4.5-platform-work-catalog-regeneration.md)) is often the completing step of another work type.

Missing INDEX row fails audit-hierarchy-depth strict checks and leaves queue item reopenable. Row includes maturity level, verify command reference, and optional promotion id trace. Consumer goals should never hunt ad hoc paths omitted from INDEX; Librarian allowed_reads assume INDEX is authoritative for playbook discovery.
